_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Free Grafts - Wolfe Grafts/Split thickness skin grafts
specialty: General Surgery, Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery, Pediatric Surgery
source_files: Free Grafts - Wolfe Grafts - Split thickness skin grafts_Medco.pdf
Free Grafts - Wolfe Grafts - Split thickness skin grafts_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Used to cover wounds where skin has been lost
Early cover with a skin graft reduces fluid, electrolyte, protein and other nutrient loss from the body
Common reasons for a skin grafting procedure include: deep burns, skin infections, loss of skin following trauma, post skin cancer surgery
Generally used to resurface small defects where cosmetic outcome is of importance
Surgically created raw areas on Face and hands

claim.label: Post procedure clinical photograph of the affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Were the Clinical notes detailing original pathology (In case of Split thickness skin graft Time of burns) submitted?
Supporting reports and Clinical photograph submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the Post procedure clinical photograph of the affected part submitted? (Optional)
Was the discharge summary report submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or clinical photograph not indicative of a full thickness skin graft for small defects on face/hands, or if applied to a large area without justification.
Supporting reports or clinical photograph missing or unclear at pre-authorization stage.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) lacking daily vitals or comprehensive treatment details at claim stage.
Operative notes not adequately detailing the surgical steps and specific characteristics of the Wolfe graft used.
Post-procedure clinical photograph of the affected part not submitted, unclear, or not showing the grafted area, or indicating a large area covered by full thickness graft.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
Restoration of an intact skin barrier is of critical importance following wound and may be achieved in numerous ways, including skin grafting.
surgical_options: Skin Grafting: Transfer of skin from one area to other without its inherent blood supply.
Skin grafts are classified as Full thickness or split thickness grafts, based on their thickness.
Full thickness Skin Graft includes both epidermis and full thickness dermis.
Donor areas for full thickness skin grafts: supraclavicular, post auricular, sub mammary, antecubital and inguinal regions.
Advantages of full thickness skin grafts: better color and texture match, less secondary contraction, better sensation and retention of sebaceous glands and hair follicles.
Procedure of grafting involves: Donor site selection, wound preparation, graft harvest, graft placement on the raw area, dressing.
contraindications: Cannot be used for large areas as take of large full thickness grafts can be problematic.
Larger donor sites cannot be closed primarily.
